Quick Reference Guide
MAN-10132-05 GeoMx® DSP Instrument (software v3.1)
Click New Data Collection or hover 
over Data Collection and select 
New/Continue Run . You may select 
Records ahead of time to define slide records and scan parameters.Start the run1
Clean bottom of slide with 70% ethanol. Ensure all residue from hydrophobic pen has been removed. Place slide evenly in slide holder, face up, label toward you. Lower the slide tray clamp. Entire tissue 
section should be visible in elongated window; cover immediately with 6 mL Buﬀer S. Record the slots in which each slide is loaded:Load the slides into the slide holder one at a time2
1 2 3 4
Load GeoMx DSP instrument3
Follow the prompts in the GeoMx DSP Control Center.
Define Scan Area5
Zoom in and out and use X– 
and Y– sliders to define the area 
the instrument should scan. Minimum Tissue slider filters particles and small areas of tissue from the image. Sensitivity slider adjusts intensity of tissue identification.
Scan6
The system will begin the 20x scan. Once complete, click the Edit ROIs button under the image. 
Select ROIs7
Create geometric shapes, custom polygons, or grids. Overlay scan images to guide ROI selection or transfer ROIs from another scan.
Minimum ROI size for protein assays: 2,000 
μm2; 20 cells per segment. For RNA-NGS 
assays: 30,000 μ m2; 100 cells per segment.

ROI selection or transfer ROIs from another scan.
Minimum ROI size for protein assays: 2,000 
μm2; 20 cells per segment. For RNA-NGS 
assays: 30,000 μ m2; 100 cells per segment. 
For RNA-nCounter assays: 30,000 μ m2; 200 
cells per segment. 
Identify slides4
Link loaded slides to slide records and scan parameters created earlier, or create new ones. Choose between Scan Only mode and Scan and Collect. Upload unzipped configuration files for all kits used in slide preparation (nanostring.com/dspconfigfiles). 
• Ensur e that you have uploaded and selected config files that 
match the name of the assays used on the slides. 
•
 If using a cus
tom panel or probes, NanoString Bioinformatics 
provides a .pkc file to be uploaded in the Probe Reagent Kit 
field in addition to other assay .pkc files.
•
 If y
ou do not see the file you need, contact 
bioinformatics@nanostring.com for assistance.
• F
or protein assays with nCounter readout, ensure selected 
core and modules have compatible Probe R spaces. See Appendix IV of GeoMx DSP Slide Preparation User Manual for more information.

For more comprehensive information, visit us at nanostring.com or NanoString University at https:/ /university.nanostring.com for user manuals and training material.Segment ROIs (optional)8
Open the Segment Menu and segment 
ROIs into biological compartments based on fluorescence signal.
Channel designations: 
• + includes the channel in the segment
• - excludes the channel in the segment
• Ø ignores the channel.
NOTE: It is often helpful to establish thresholds 
first: Use one segment definition and set it to + for one channel at a time. Adjust that channel until it covers the desired area.
Make changes (if needed)9
Use the Scan Workspace menus to 
edit or delete ROIs. You can also export images.
Finalize and unload GeoMx instrument11

Use the Scan Workspace menus to 
edit or delete ROIs. You can also export images.
Finalize and unload GeoMx instrument11
1. Once Collection is complete, choose New Data Collection (to launch a new collection with eitherthe pr
esent slides or plate) or Remove Slides and Microplate. Follow the instrument prompts. Store
protein slides in 1X TBST at 4°C up to 1 day; mount in aqueous media for longer storage. Store RNAslides in 2X SSC at 4°C for up to 21 days. Protect slides from light.
2.
Click on the plat
e icon area at the lower right of the GeoMx DSP Control Center to open the Finalize
Plate window.
• Reboot instrument weekly and before beginning a run.
• When ins
trument is powered off, open Reagent Bottle Bay and check connections of QuickConnect cap fittings. Do not overtighten the waste bottle cap.
• Lea
ve the clean, dry slide holder inside the instrument at the end of a run.
• Follo
w Hibernation Protocol if instrument will be inactive for >2 weeks. Exit Scan Workspace & begin ROI collection 10
Click on the Exit Scan Workspace button and Approve ROIs.

For NGS readout: 
• Click Readout Group and pick the appropriate one for your plate. 
• Select the Illumina pla
tform from the Counting Device Model menu.
• Choose Single or Pair
ed Read.
• Ent
er read lengths (2 x 27bp is recommended) into the Read 1 Length and Read 2 Length fields.
• Establish which GeoMx Seq C
ode or Pro Code plate should be associated with which Plate 
Barcode in the Readout Group Information grid.
• Click the Updat
e button when all fields have been filled in properly. 
• Select the Finaliz
e and Download Readout Package button. 
• Proc
eed to NGS Readout. For nCounter readout:

P
ack lot #, enter it here 
and click Update.
